label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is wrong according to the news article provided. First, I need to go through each option and check against the text. Option A says that the US will give up the space program because of the accident. The article mentions that President Bush said, "America's space program will go on," so this statement is false. Therefore, option A is incorrect according to the news. Option B states that NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster. The article says that NASA said it was too early to nail down a precise cause. So this is correct, and B is not the wrong statement. Option C says that before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded during landing. The article states that in 42 years of US human space flight, there had never been an accident during descent to Earth or landing. The Challenger exploded on take-off, not during landing. So C
